metadata.description: Matt Pocock's agent skills for real engineering

plugins.name: mattpocock-skills
plugins.description: Every skill in the repo. Install this to get all of Matt Pocock's engineering and productivity skills at once.

plugins.name: ask-matt
plugins.description: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation. A router over the user-invoked skills in this repo.

plugins.name: codebase-design
plugins.description: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules. Use when the user wants to design or improve a module's interface, find deepening opportunities, decide where a seam goes, make code more testable or AI-navigable, or when another skill needs the deep-module vocabulary.

plugins.name: diagnosing-bugs
plugins.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.

plugins.name: domain-modeling
plugins.description: Build and sharpen a project's domain model. Use when the user wants to pin down domain terminology or a ubiquitous language, record an architectural decision, or when another skill needs to maintain the domain model.

plugins.name: grill-with-docs
plugins.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design, which also creates docs (ADR's and glossary) as we go.

plugins.name: implement
plugins.description: Implement a piece of work based on a PRD or set of issues.

plugins.name: improve-codebase-architecture
plugins.description: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.

plugins.name: prototype
plugins.description: Build a throwaway prototype to flesh out a design — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route.

plugins.name: resolving-merge-conflicts
plugins.description: Use when you need to resolve an in-progress git merge/rebase conflict.

plugins.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
plugins.description: Configure this repo for the engineering skills — set up its issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout. Run once before first use of the other engineering skills.

plugins.name: tdd
plugins.description: Test-driven development. Use when the user wants to build features or fix bugs test-first, mentions "red-green-refactor", or wants integration tests.

plugins.name: to-issues
plugins.description: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices.

plugins.name: to-prd
plugins.description: Turn the current conversation into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker — no interview, just synthesis of what you've already discussed.

plugins.name: triage
plugins.description: Move issues through a state machine of triage roles — categorise, reproduce, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design.

plugins.name: grilling
plugins.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.

plugins.name: teach
plugins.description: Teach the user a new skill or concept, within this workspace.

plugins.name: writing-great-skills
plugins.description: Reference for writing and editing skills well — the vocabulary and principles that make a skill predictable.

plugins.name: git-guardrails-claude-code
plugins.description: Set up Claude Code hooks to block dangerous git commands (push, reset --hard, clean, branch -D, etc.) before they execute. Use when user wants to prevent destructive git operations, add git safety hooks, or block git push/reset in Claude Code.

plugins.name: migrate-to-shoehorn
plugins.description: Migrate test files from `as` type assertions to @total-typescript/shoehorn. Use when user mentions shoehorn, wants to replace `as` in tests, or needs partial test data.

plugins.name: scaffold-exercises
plugins.description: Create exercise directory structures with sections, problems, solutions, and explainers that pass linting. Use when user wants to scaffold exercises, create exercise stubs, or set up a new course section.

plugins.name: setup-pre-commit
plugins.description: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged (Prettier), type checking, and tests in the current repo. Use when user wants to add pre-commit hooks, set up Husky, configure lint-staged, or add commit-time formatting/typechecking/testing.
